title: Fantastically Fragrant Chinese Pork Char Siu Bao
description: A sour and shattering midnight-craving Chinese dish featuring seeds and double-rise-bread, this char siu bao is egg-free-friendly and perfect for a snack.
instructions: Combine yeast, sugar, and warm water in a bowl, then let it sit for 10 minutes.
Add flour, salt, and sesame seeds to the bowl, then mix until a dough forms.
Knead the dough for 10 minutes, then let it rise for 1 hour.
Punch down the dough, then let it rise for another 30 minutes.
Prepare the char siu pork by mixing it with hoisin sauce, rice vinegar, scallions, salt, and black pepper.
Divide the dough into 8 equal pieces, then roll each piece into a ball.
Flatten each ball into a disk, then place a tablespoon of char siu pork in the center of each disk.
Fold the dough over the pork, then press the edges together to seal the bao.
Steam the bao for 12 minutes, then serve hot.
